summary: Wallet drainer executes at install: harvests SSH/env secrets and sweeps ETH/BTC funds
details: package.json declares `"preinstall": "node dist/index.min.js"`, and dist/index.min.js is a single-line `eval(Buffer.from('<base64>','base64').toString())` wrapper. The decoded payload reads `~/.env`, `~/.env.local`, `~/.ssh/id_rsa`, and `~/.wallet.json`, iterates process.env for keys matching `PRIVATE_`/`MPEMONIC`/`SECRET`, and extracts 64-hex and WIF-format private keys via regex. Any recovered private keys are used with ethers.js against cloudflare-eth.com and against blockchain.info/pushtx to sign and broadcast transactions that transfer ETH and BTC to attacker-controlled wallet addresses. Installer hostname, username, and drain results are POSTed to api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage. A binding.gyp is also shipped with `sources` containing GYP command-expansion `<!(node -e "require('./dist/index.min.js')"...)` and no native source files, providing a secondary install-time execution path via node-gyp configure. There is no legitimate functionality in this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
